briefdescription.para: enumeration texture types
Default flags for slider() and drag() below.

detaileddescription.para: Draws a label on top of the viewport area. id should be a unique ID persistent across frames.

Shows ImGui popup that suggests user to save changes, user need to call ImGui::OpenPopup( str_id ) to open this popup. It has 3 options: save, don't save, cancel str_id - ImGui string id for the popup window settings - settings for dialog

briefdescription.para: Returns the global UI scale. Use this instead of passing around the scale in parameters.
Checks if the item with this name in the current window is active.
get texture by type
init internal parameters
returns imgui modifier Id based on current environment
returns "Ctrl" by default or "Command" if ImGui has changed it internally for macos
returns true if button is pressed in this frame, preserve its further processing in viewer keyboard events system if taken here

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, bool active, const Vector2f &size=Vector2f(0, 0), ImGuiButtonFlags flags=ImGuiButtonFlags_None, const ButtonCustomizationParams &customParams={})

briefdescription.para: draw gradient button, which can be disabled (active = false)
draw gradient button, which can be customized

detaileddescription.para: draw gradient button, which can be disabled (active = false) returns true if button is clicked in this frame, or key is pressed (optional)
draw gradient button returns true if button is clicked in this frame, or key is pressed (optional)
draw gradient button with the ordinary button size returns true if button is clicked in this frame, or key is pressed (optional)

briefdescription.para: draw button with same logic as radioButton
draw dash with text along the horizontal axis
draw dash with text along the vertical axis

briefdescription.para: button with a gradient, always active

briefdescription.para: button without a gradient, always active, configurable by an external style

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::string &iconName, const Vector2f &iconSize, const std::string &text, const ImVec2 &buttonSize, int *value, int ownValue, bool textUnderIcon=true, ImGuiKey key=ImGuiKey_None)
memberdef.name: buttonUniqueIcon

detaileddescription.para: draw button with icon same logic as radioButton the colors of the internal style are used

briefdescription.para: draws checkbox-like toggle (enabled/disabled states)(O=)/(=O)
draw gradient checkbox
If valueOverride is specified, then the checkbox is disabled and that value is displayed instead of value.
If valid is false checkbox is disabled. Same as checkboxOrFixedValue( ..., valid ? nullopt : false ).
draw gradient checkbox with mixed state
draw gradient checkbox

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, Getter get, Setter set, bool valid)
memberdef.name: checkboxValid

briefdescription.para: Draw a checkbox toggling one or more bits in the mask.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, CheckboxOrModifierState &value, int modifiers, int respectedModifiers=-1, std::optional< bool > valueOverride={})
memberdef.name: checkboxOrModifier

detaileddescription.para: Draws a checkbox, that gets inverted while a modifier key is held. Use value's operator bool to read the final value. (E.g. if (value), and so on.) modifiers must be one or more of ImGuiMod_{Shift,Alt,Ctrl}. By default ignores all modifiers not in modifiers. But if respectedModifiers is specified, then only ignores modifiers not included in it. respectedModifiers must be a superset of modifiers. -1 has special meaning, making it same as modifiers (which makes it have no effect). In other words, modifiers are checked like this: mask = respectedModifiers != -1 ? respectedModifiers : modifiers; modifiersHeld = (ImGui::GetIO().KeyMods & mask) == modifiers; If valueOverride is specified, then acts as checkboxOrFixedValue: disables the checkbox and displays that value instead of the real one, and also pretends that the modifier isn't held.

briefdescription.para: draw gradient radio button

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, int *value, int valButton, std::optional< int > valueOverride)
memberdef.name: radioButtonOrFixedValue

briefdescription.para: If valueOverride is specified, then the radio button is disabled and that value is displayed instead of value.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, RadioButtonOrModifierState &value, int valButton, int modifiers, int respectedModifiers=-1, std::optional< int > valueOverride={})
memberdef.name: radioButtonOrModifier

detaileddescription.para: Draws a radio button, that can be affected by modifier keys. modifiers must be one or more of ImGuiMod_{Shift,Alt,Ctrl}. By default ignores all modifiers not in modifiers. But if respectedModifiers is specified, then only ignores modifiers not included in it. respectedModifiers must be a superset of modifiers. -1 has special meaning, making it same as modifiers (which makes it have no effect). If valueOverride is specified, then acts as radioButtonOrFixedValue: disables the radio button and displays that value instead of the real one, and also pretends that the modifier isn't held.

briefdescription.para: draw gradient color edit 4

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, int *v, const std::vector< std::string > &options, bool showPreview=true, const std::vector< std::string > &tooltips={}, const std::string &defaultText="Not selected")
memberdef.name: combo

briefdescription.para: draw combo box

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, const std::string &text="Not selected", bool showPreview=true)
memberdef.name: beginCombo

briefdescription.para: draw custom content combo box

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, std::string &str, ImGuiInputTextFlags flags=0, ImGuiInputTextCallback callback=nullptr, void *user_data=nullptr)
memberdef.name: inputText

briefdescription.para: Draws text input, should be used instead of ImGui::InputText().

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, char *array, std::size_t size, ImGuiInputTextFlags flags=0, ImGuiInputTextCallback callback=nullptr, void *user_data=nullptr)
memberdef.name: inputTextIntoArray

briefdescription.para: This overload is for arrays, as opposed to std::strings.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, std::string &str, const ImVec2 &size=ImVec2(), ImGuiInputTextFlags flags=0, ImGuiInputTextCallback callback=nullptr, void *user_data=nullptr)

briefdescription.para: Draws multiline text input, should be used instead of ImGui::InputTextMultiline().

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, char *buf, size_t buf_size, const ImVec2 &size=ImVec2(), ImGuiInputTextFlags flags=0, ImGuiInputTextCallback callback=nullptr, void *user_data=nullptr)
memberdef.name: inputTextIntoArrayMultiline

briefdescription.para: This overload is for arrays, as opposed to std::strings.

memberdef.argsstring: (CachedTextSize &cache, const char *label, std::string &str, const ImVec2 &size=ImVec2(), ImGuiInputTextFlags flags=0, ImGuiInputTextCallback callback=nullptr, void *user_data=nullptr)
memberdef.name: inputTextMultilineFullyScrollable

briefdescription.para: This version adds a horizontal scrollbar. Also it never draws the label, and uses full window width by default (so you can pass 0 as width).

memberdef.argsstring: (CachedTextSize &cache, const char *label, char *buf, size_t buf_size, const ImVec2 &size=ImVec2(), ImGuiInputTextFlags flags=0, ImGuiInputTextCallback callback=nullptr, void *user_data=nullptr)
memberdef.name: inputTextIntoArrayMultilineFullyScrollable

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, std::string &str, float width=0.0f, ImGuiInputTextFlags flags=0, ImGuiInputTextCallback callback=nullptr, void *user_data=nullptr)
memberdef.name: inputTextCentered

briefdescription.para: draw input text box with text aligned by center

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, const std::string &str, float width=0.0f, const std::optional< ImVec4 > &textColor={}, const std::optional< ImVec4 > &labelColor={})
memberdef.name: inputTextCenteredReadOnly

briefdescription.para: draw read-only text box with text aligned by center
Default drag speed for UI::drag().
Default step speed for UI::input().

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, T &v, const U &vMin, const U &vMax, UnitToStringParams< E > unitParams={}, ImGuiSliderFlags flags=defaultSliderFlags)
memberdef.name: slider

detaileddescription.para: Draw a slider. E must be specified explicitly, to one of: NoUnit LengthUnit, AngleUnit, ... By default, for angles v will be converted to degrees for display (but vMin, vMax are still in radians, same as v), while length and unit-less values will be left as is. This can be customized in unitParams or globally (see MRUnits.h).

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, T &v, SpeedType vSpeed=getDefaultDragSpeed< E, SpeedType >(), const U &vMin=std::numeric_limits< U >::lowest(), const U &vMax=std::numeric_limits< U >::max(), UnitToStringParams< E > unitParams={}, ImGuiSliderFlags flags=defaultSliderFlags, const U &step=getDefaultStep< E, U, T >(false), const U &stepFast=getDefaultStep< E, U, T >(true))
memberdef.name: drag

detaileddescription.para: Draw a dragging widget. Also includes [+] and [-] buttons (for integers only by default_, like ImGui::Input). E must be specified explicitly, to one of: NoUnit LengthUnit, AngleUnit, ... By default, for angles v will be converted to degrees for display (but vSpeed is still in radians, same as v), while length and unit-less values will be left as is. This can be customized in unitParams or globally (see MRUnits.h). If only the min limit is specified, then the max limit is assumed to be the largest number.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, T &v, const U &vMin=std::numeric_limits< U >::lowest(), const U &vMax=std::numeric_limits< U >::max(), UnitToStringParams< E > unitParams={}, ImGuiSliderFlags flags=defaultSliderFlags)
memberdef.name: input

briefdescription.para: Like drag(), but clicking it immediately activates text input, so it's not actually draggable.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, const T &v, std::optional< ImVec4 > textColor={}, UnitToStringParams< E > unitParams={}, std::optional< ImVec4 > labelColor={})
memberdef.name: readOnlyValue

detaileddescription.para: Draw a read-only copyable value. E must be specified explicitly, to one of: NoUnit LengthUnit, AngleUnit, ... By default, for angles v will be converted to degrees for display, while length and unit-less values will be left as is. This can be customized in unitParams or globally (see MRUnits.h).

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, T &plus, T &minus, UnitToStringParams< E > unitToStringParams, F &&func)
memberdef.name: plusMinusGeneric

detaileddescription.para: A generic wrapper for drawing plus-minus drags/inputs/etc that can be toggled between symmetric and asymmetric mode. Prefer inputPlusMinus() and other functions written on top of this one. func is a (const char* subLabel, T& value, const UnitToStringParams<E>& subUnitParams) -> bool, draw your widget there with the automatic width, the specified label and value, and return its return value. value will receive either plus or minus.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, T &plus, T &minus, SpeedType speed=getDefaultDragSpeed< E, SpeedType >(), T plusMin=T{}, T plusMax=std::numeric_limits< T >::max(), UnitToStringParams< E > unitParams={}, ImGuiSliderFlags flags=defaultSliderFlags, F &&wrapFunc=nullptr)
memberdef.name: dragPlusMinus

detaileddescription.para: A drag() for two numbers that can be toggled between symmetric and asymmetric mode. Notice the wrapFunc parameter. If specified, it is a (const char* subLabel, T& value, auto&& f) -> bool lambda that must do return f();. That is called for each of the two plus/minus parts of the widget, and lets you e.g. set a custom style for those. Here we intentionally don't allow passing the step values, that wouldn't render too well.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, T &plus, T &minus, T plusMin=T{}, T plusMax=std::numeric_limits< T >::max(), UnitToStringParams< E > unitParams={}, ImGuiSliderFlags flags=defaultSliderFlags, F &&wrapFunc=nullptr)
memberdef.name: inputPlusMinus

detaileddescription.para: An input() for two numbers that can be toggled between symmetric and asymmetric mode. Notice the wrapFunc parameter. If specified, it is a (const char* subLabel, T& value, auto&& f) -> bool lambda that must do return f();. That is called for each of the two plus/minus parts of the widget, and lets you e.g. set a custom style for those.

memberdef.type: MRVIEWER_API const std::pair< const char *, ImU32 > &
memberdef.definition: MRVIEWER_API const std::pair< const char *, ImU32 > & MR::UI::notificationChar
memberdef.name: notificationChar

briefdescription.para: returns icons font character for given notification type, and its color
draws hint with corresponding mouse btn icon
similar to ImGui::Text but use current text color with alpha channel = 0.5
similar to ImGui::TextWrapped but use current text color with alpha channel = 0.5
similar to ImGui::TextWrapped but also have styled background and notification type indicator
draw tooltip only if current item is hovered
separator line with customizations

detaileddescription.para: add text with separator line if issueCount is greater than zero, this number will be displayed in red color after the text. If it equals zero - in green color Otherwise it will not be displayed
separator line with icon and text iconSize icon size without scaling
draws progress bar note that even while scaling is given by argument size should still respect it size: x(y) < 0 - take all available width(height) x(y) == 0 - use default width(height)

briefdescription.para: create and append items into a TabBar: see corresponding ImGui:: functions

detaileddescription.para: Sets the vertical text position to match the text of a specific control on the same line The control is assumed to contain standard one-line text with equal padding from top and bottom edges The padding is control-specific Sample usage: UI::alignTextToFramePadding( ImGui::GetStyle().FramePadding.y ); /// (with this value, it is equivalent to ImGui::AlignTextToFramePadding() ) ImGui::Text( "Button:" ); ImGui::SameLine(); UI::buttonCommonSize( "Button" ); /// the control we align to

Sets the vertical text position to match the text of a specific control on the same line Same as alignTextToFramePadding, but takes the full control height Can be used, for example, for UI::button with nondefault height

briefdescription.para: Specialization of alignTextToFramePadding for UI::radioButton.
Specialization of alignTextToFramePadding for UI::checkbox.
Specialization of alignTextToFramePadding for UI::button with default height.

detaileddescription.para: Select the background of the part of the current window from min to max. If the min is not set, then the current position is taken.If max is not set, then the end of the window is taken. Added some indentation if min or max is not set.

briefdescription.para: Draw modal window to save user configs (for example Palettes)
